title: rc.5 coverage gate + dogfood test
description: Add scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs mirroring rc.4 coverage gate pattern. Gate checks: (a) MCP tool registry contains 4 tools (no fab_get_rule_sections); (b) server name = fabric-knowledge-server; (c) agents.meta nodes lack 5 dead fields; (d) plan_context output lacks 4 dead fields, gains candidates_full_content; (e) deleted files absent: services/annotate-intent.ts, audit-log.ts, human-lock-or-equivalent.ts, api/intent.ts, .fabric/audit.jsonl, .fabric/forensic.json, packages/dashboard/, templates/, examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/, 思路.md; (f) renamed server files present; (g) hooks configs present for 3 clients; (h) fabric plan-context-hint CLI subcommand works; (i) lint #22-#25 registered. After gate passes, run full dogfood test cycle: complete archive→review→consume→lint cycle end-to-end with new fields on Fabric self repo.

files.change: Implement 9-check gate script following rc.4 pattern

implementation: Read existing rc.4 coverage gate script (scripts/rc4-coverage-gate.mjs or similar) as template
Implement each check as a function returning { name, passed, details }
Check (a) MCP tool registry: import createFabricServer + inspect registered tool names
Check (b) server name: grep packages/server/src/index.ts for 'fabric-knowledge-server'
Check (c) agents.meta schema: import agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema; assert removed fields
Check (d) plan_context output: import schema; assert removed + new fields
Check (e) deleted files: fs.existsSync on each deleted path returns false
Check (f) renamed files: fs.existsSync on each renamed path returns true
Check (g) hook configs: fs.existsSync claude-code.json + codex-hooks.json + cursor-hooks.json; parse + validate Stop hook
Check (h) CLI subcommand: spawn `pnpm exec fabric plan-context-hint --all` from script; parse JSON
Check (i) lint checks: import doctor inspectors; verify #22-#25 functions exported
Aggregate results; exit 0 if all pass, exit 1 with summary table if any fail

Run dogfood: from clean Fabric repo, invoke fabric init → fabric-archive skill on a sample session → fab_review.list → approve → fab_get_knowledge_sections (verify knowledge_consumed event) → fabric doctor (verify lint #23-#25 trigger correctly)
Document dogfood test cycle results in commit message or rc.5 release notes

reference.examples: Mirror rc.4 structural assertions + dogfood flow

criteria: scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs passes following rc.4 coverage gate pattern
All 9 structural checks pass on rc.5 head commit
Dogfood test on Fabric self repo: complete archive→review→consume→lint cycle end-to-end with new fields
rc.5 tag created on green gate + green dogfood

rationale.chosen_approach: Structural assertion script + manual dogfood verification (mirror rc.4 pattern)
decision_factors: rc.4 established pattern: script for structural, dogfood for behavioral
rationale.tradeoffs: Dogfood is non-reproducible; mitigated by structural gate covering 90% of regressions

manual_checks: Manual dogfood cycle on test repo (or this repo) completes without errors
events.jsonl shows expected event sequence (knowledge_proposed → knowledge_approved → knowledge_consumed)
success_metrics: Dogfood end-to-end success on at least 1 sample session

risks.description: Dogfood reveals integration gaps not caught by structural gate
risks.mitigation: Run dogfood early in coverage-gate task; loop fixes back into specific rc.5 tasks
risks.fallback: Patch in rc.5.1 if behavior issues surface
